tasks.name: Amazon: Filtered Product Intelligence
tasks.prompt: Navigate to https://www.amazon.com and search for "wireless ergonomic mouse". Apply filters for 4 Stars & Up and Prime Eligible. Sort results by Price: Low to High. Open the first non-sponsored product listing. Extract and report: (1) product title, (2) listed price, (3) star rating, (4) total number of ratings, and (5) earliest delivery date shown. Save a screenshot to eval-amazon-task.png before finishing. Return the final answer in exactly five labeled lines: Title:, Price:, Rating:, Reviews:, Delivery:.

tasks.name: LinkedIn: Company Page Data Extraction
tasks.prompt: Navigate to https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft/. If a sign-in prompt appears, dismiss it and continue on the public company page. Extract and report: (1) company name, (2) number of followers, and (3) industry. Save a screenshot to eval-linkedin-task.png before finishing. Return the final answer in exactly three labeled lines: Company:, Followers:, Industry:.

tasks.name: GitHub: Repository Release Intelligence
tasks.prompt: Navigate to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode. Open the Releases section and then open the latest release page. Extract and report: (1) release tag, (2) release title, (3) published relative time/date text, and (4) total number of reactions shown on that release page. Save a screenshot to eval-github-release.png before finishing. Return the final answer in exactly four labeled lines: Tag:, Title:, Published:, Reactions:.

tasks.name: Wikipedia: Multi-Field Infobox Extraction
tasks.prompt: Navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JavaScript. From the infobox, extract and report: (1) designed by, (2) first appeared year, (3) stable release name/version text, and (4) filename extension entry. Save a screenshot to eval-wikipedia-js.png before finishing. Return the final answer in exactly four labeled lines: DesignedBy:, FirstAppeared:, StableRelease:, Extension:.

tasks.name: Stack Overflow: Tagged Questions Snapshot
tasks.prompt: Navigate to https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/python?tab=Newest. Open the first question in the list. Extract and report: (1) question title, (2) vote count, (3) answer count, and (4) the first tag shown on that question. Save a screenshot to eval-stackoverflow-question.png before finishing. Return the final answer in exactly four labeled lines: Title:, Votes:, Answers:, FirstTag:.
